questions.q: From which city did the Pallava dynasty rule?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas ruled from their capital at Kanchipuram in present-day Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: The Pallavas are especially renowned as great patrons of what?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas were celebrated patrons of temple architecture and learning.

questions.q: The famous Pallava monuments are located at which coastal site?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas built renowned monuments at Mahabalipuram, including the Shore Temple.

questions.q: The Pallavas ruled approximately between which centuries CE?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas ruled from roughly the 4th to the 9th centuries CE.

questions.q: Kanchipuram, the Pallava capital, is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram lies in present-day Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: The Shore Temple, a famous Pallava monument, stands beside which body of water?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram stands on the coast of the Bay of Bengal.

questions.q: The Pallavas patronised learning in which two languages?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas were patrons of both Tamil and Sanskrit learning.

questions.q: Many Pallava monuments at Mahabalipuram are of which architectural type?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas pioneered rock-cut shrines and monolithic temples at Mahabalipuram.

questions.q: The Pallavas are described as architects of which region of India?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas are remembered as great architects of southern India.

questions.q: Mahabalipuram is also known by which other name?
questions.explanation: Mahabalipuram is also called Mamallapuram, named after the Pallava king Narasimhavarman 'Mamalla'.

questions.q: Which Pallava king, a contemporary of Pulakeshin II, was a playwright and pioneer of rock-cut architecture?
questions.explanation: Mahendravarman I was a noted patron, dramatist, and pioneer of Pallava rock-cut temples.

questions.q: Which Pallava king sacked the Chalukya capital Badami and took the title Vatapikonda?
questions.explanation: Narasimhavarman I captured Badami from the Chalukyas around 642 CE, earning the title Vatapikonda.

questions.q: The chief dynastic rivals of the Pallavas in the Deccan were which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Pallavas and the Chalukyas of Badami were long-standing rivals for Deccan supremacy.

questions.q: The Pallava king who built the Kailasanatha temple at Kanchipuram was which ruler?
questions.explanation: Rajasimha (Narasimhavarman II) built the celebrated Kailasanatha temple at Kanchipuram.

questions.q: The grand rock relief at Mahabalipuram is commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The vast open-air relief at Mahabalipuram is known as the Descent of the Ganges or Arjuna's Penance.

questions.q: The monolithic temple-chariots carved at Mahabalipuram are popularly called what?
questions.explanation: The five monolithic shrines at Mahabalipuram are known as the Pancha Rathas (five chariots).

questions.q: Which king is traditionally credited with founding the imperial line of the Pallavas in the late 6th century, before Mahendravarman I?
questions.explanation: Simhavishnu is credited with founding the great line of the imperial Pallavas in the late 6th century CE.

questions.q: The Pallava style of architecture is often classed as an early phase of which broader tradition?
questions.explanation: Pallava architecture is regarded as a formative phase of the Dravidian style of South Indian temples.

questions.q: The Pallavas had important trade and cultural contacts across the seas with which region?
questions.explanation: Pallava art and culture influenced Southeast Asia, with which the dynasty maintained maritime contact.

questions.q: The later Pallava king Nandivarman II belonged to a collateral branch and is associated with which Kanchipuram temple?
questions.explanation: Nandivarman II Pallavamalla built the Vaikunta Perumal temple at Kanchipuram.

questions.q: Which dynasty finally ended Pallava rule in the late 9th century CE?
questions.explanation: The rising Cholas under Aditya I overthrew the last Pallavas in the late 9th century CE.

questions.q: Mahendravarman I authored which Sanskrit farce, demonstrating Pallava literary patronage?
questions.explanation: Mahendravarman I wrote the satirical Sanskrit farce Mattavilasa Prahasana.

questions.q: The Pallava grants and inscriptions distinguish an earlier line; the famous early copper-plate charters were often issued in which script?
questions.explanation: Early Pallava charters used Prakrit and Sanskrit in Pallava-Grantha script, important in the evolution of South Indian writing.

questions.q: Narasimhavarman I aided which exiled Sri Lankan prince to regain his throne, recorded in the chronicles?
questions.explanation: Narasimhavarman I helped the Sri Lankan prince Manavarma, sending naval expeditions to restore him.

questions.q: The Pallava king Dantivarman and his successors faced pressure from the resurgent Pandyas and which Deccan power in the 8th-9th centuries?
questions.explanation: Later Pallavas were squeezed between the Pandyas and the powerful Rashtrakutas of the Deccan.

questions.q: The Pallava architectural sequence is often grouped into phases named after kings; the rock-cut cave phase is named after which ruler?
questions.explanation: The earliest rock-cut cave temples are classed as the Mahendra style, after Mahendravarman I.

questions.q: Which Saiva saint-poets (Nayanars) and Vaishnava Alvars flourished partly under Pallava patronage, shaping the Bhakti movement in the Tamil land?
options: Appar, Sambandar, and the early Alvars
questions.explanation: Nayanars such as Appar and Sambandar and the Alvars were active in the Pallava era, fostering Tamil Bhakti devotion.

questions.q: The last significant Pallava ruler, defeated by the Chola Aditya I around 897 CE, was named what?
questions.explanation: Aparajita Pallava was defeated by the Chola Aditya I, effectively ending Pallava power.

questions.q: Nandivarman II Pallavamalla was raised to the throne from a collateral Pallava line based in which place?
options: Kadava / the line descended from Bhimavarman
questions.explanation: Nandivarman II came from a collateral branch (descended from Bhimavarman), chosen by ministers to continue the line.

questions.q: The Pallava influence on Southeast Asian architecture is seen in the temples of which kingdom, which borrowed the Pallava-Grantha script?
options: The kingdoms of Cambodia and Java (e.g. Khmer, Sailendra)
questions.explanation: Pallava-Grantha script and temple forms influenced Cambodian and Javanese kingdoms in Southeast Asia.
